samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits. Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits. Parameter describing the bit-width of the multiplicand and multiplier, with a value of 8. Register to store the input enable signal, mul_en_in, and control the multiplication operation. Register to store the input multiplicand, mul_a. Register to store the input multiplier, mul_b. Register to accumulate the partial sums. Register to store the final product value. Wire to store the partial products. On the positive edge of the clock signal, the module samples the input enable signal and stores it in a register, mul_en_out_reg. This register holds the input enable signal value for the next clock cycle. On the positive edge of the clock signal, the module samples the input values and stores them in the corresponding registers, mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg. These registers hold the input multiplicand and multiplier values for the multiplication operation. The module generates partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. It uses conditional assignments to select the appropriate bits from the multiplicand based on the multiplier bits. The partial products are stored in a set of wires, temp, with each wire representing a different bit position. The module performs addition operations on the partial products to calculate partial sums. It uses a set of registers, sum, to store the intermediate sum values. Each register corresponds to a group of partial products and is updated on the positive edge of the clock signal. The module sums up all the partial sums to obtain the final product. It uses a register, mul_out_reg, to store the accumulated sum. On the positive edge of the clock signal, the register is updated with the sum of all partial sums. The module assigns the output product value, mul_out, based on the output enable signal and the value in mul_out_reg. If the output enable signal is active, indicating a valid product, the value in mul_out_reg is assigned to mul_out. Otherwise, mul_out is set to 0.
